message:
The other day, I found myself in a situation that made me feel like a superhero of preparedness. My neighbor, frazzled and on the verge of a mini-crisis, had forgotten to charge her phone before heading out for the day. With a storm rolling in, she was worried about her kids at home. My heart went out to her, but instead of panicking, I pulled out my trusty go-bag — stocked with snacks, a portable charger, and even a few fun activities to keep the kids entertained. I was finally the prepared one for once! 

We ended up having a little impromptu play-date while the storm passed, and it felt great to be able to help. It’s funny how a little bit of planning can turn a stressful moment into a chance for connection. Have you ever had a moment where being prepared made a difference for you or someone else?
